summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened the sermon by addressing the common struggle of living a 'half in, half out' Christian life, using the imagery of having one foot in God's yard and one in the world's. He grounded the message in James 1:6-8, explaining that divided loyalty leads to an unstable life where we cannot expect to receive from the Lord. Pastor Travis connected this to Jesus's teaching in Matthew 6:24 about being unable to serve both God and money, emphasizing that our primary pursuit must be God, not wealth. He challenged the congregation to examine whether they are filled with the Spirit of God or the spirit of greed, noting that true generosity comes from God. The core of the message provided three practical steps to move from a wavering faith to a focused, all-in commitment. First, we must identify our God-given priorities, with our relationship with Christ as number one. Second, we must learn to focus laser-like on those priorities, resisting the enemy's distractions. Third, we must develop the spiritual gift of discipline, training ourselves in godliness just as an athlete trains for a competition. Pastor Travis closed by encouraging everyone to make a fresh commitment to go 'all in' for Jesus, just as He went all in for us on the cross.
mainPoints: Identify your God-given priorities, putting your relationship with Christ first.
Develop laser focus on your priorities to resist distraction and increase your spiritual power.
Cultivate the gift of spiritual discipline, training yourself for godliness as an athlete trains for a prize.

versesMentioned.text: But when you ask, you must believe and not doubt, because the one who doubts is like a wave of the sea, blown and tossed by the wind. That person should not expect to receive anything from the Lord. Such a person is double-minded and unstable in all they do.
No one can serve two masters. Either you will hate the one and love the other, or you will be devoted to the one and despise the other. You cannot serve both God and money.

As they were walking along the road, a man said to him, 'I will follow you wherever you go.' Jesus replied, 'Foxes have dens and birds have nests, but the Son of Man has no place to lay his head.' He said to another man, 'Follow me.' But he replied, 'Lord, first let me go and bury my father.' Jesus said to him, 'Let the dead bury their own dead, but you go and proclaim the kingdom of God.' Still another said, 'I will follow you, Lord; but first let me go back and say goodbye to my family.' Jesus replied, 'No one who puts a hand to the plow and looks back is fit for service in the kingdom of God.'
But seek first his kingdom and his righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well.

Have nothing to do with godless myths and old wives’ tales; rather, train yourself to be godly. For physical training is of some value, but godliness has value for all things, holding promise for both the present life and the life to come.

Do you not know that in a race all the runners run, but only one gets the prize? Run in such a way as to get the prize. Everyone who competes in the games goes into strict training. They do it to get a crown that will not last, but we do it to get a crown that will last forever. Therefore I do not run like someone running aimlessly; I do not fight like a boxer beating the air. No, I strike a blow to my body and make it my slave so that after I have preached to others, I myself will not be disqualified for the prize.

Monday.scripture: James 1:6-8 (NIV) - ...a double-minded person is unstable in all they do.
Monday.action: Identify one area of your life where you feel 'half in, half out' with God. Write it down.
Monday.prayer: Lord, reveal any divided loyalty in my heart. I want to be stable and wholehearted in my faith. Help me to trust You completely today.

Tuesday.scripture: Matthew 6:24 (NIV) - No one can serve two masters...
Tuesday.action: Evaluate your relationship with money and possessions. Are you using them for God's purposes, or are they using you? Make one decision today that puts God first in your finances.
Tuesday.prayer: Father, I choose to serve You alone. Show me if I am chasing money more than I am chasing You. I declare that You are my Master and my greatest treasure.

Wednesday.scripture: Matthew 6:33 (NIV) - But seek first his kingdom and his righteousness...
Wednesday.action: Draw your 'priority circle' as Pastor Travis described. List your top 3-5 God-given priorities. Say 'no' to one request today that would pull you away from them.
Wednesday.prayer: God, help me to seek Your kingdom above all else today. Align my schedule, my energy, and my heart with what matters most to You.

Thursday.scripture: Luke 9:62 (NIV) - No one who puts a hand to the plow and looks back is fit for service...
Thursday.action: Identify one distraction that consistently pulls your focus from God. Create a simple plan to minimize that distraction during your prayer or Bible time tomorrow.
Thursday.prayer: Jesus, I want to follow You without looking back. Forgive me for my excuses. Give me the strength to keep my eyes fixed on You and the work You have for me.

Friday.action: Commit to a 15-minute 'spiritual workout' today. Spend 5 minutes in prayer, 5 minutes reading Scripture, and 5 minutes in silent reflection or worship.
Friday.prayer: Holy Spirit, train me in godliness. I want my inner spirit to be strong and healthy. Help me develop a consistent routine that builds my faith for eternity.

Saturday.scripture: 1 Corinthians 9:25 (NIV) - Everyone who competes in the games goes into strict training...
Saturday.action: Reflect on the past week. What was one victory in your focus or discipline? What was one challenge? Prepare your heart for worship tomorrow by thanking God for His grace in both.
Saturday.prayer: God, thank You for the grace to grow. I run this race for an eternal prize. Discipline my heart so I can be a champion for Christ, living all-in for Your glory.
